body: PT BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk (BFIN) has affirmed its commitment to pay off its shelf registration bond, Series B, worth IDR 227 billion, which will mature on January 27, 2025, with funds for principal and interest repayment fully prepared from the company's internal funds. The bond, rated A+ by PT Fitch Rating Indonesia, is expected to strengthen investor confidence and maintain BFI Finance's reputation in the financing industry.
title: BFI Finance Indonesia to Repay IDR 227 Billion Bond Ahead of Maturity Date

body: PT Chitose Internasional Tbk (CHIT) gained two new individual investors, Kiky Dedie Suherlan and Henoch Weylim, who acquired 5% and 6.12% shares, respectively, while PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk (ELMT) acquired 64.06 million shares of PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA). Meanwhile, PT Kalbe Farma Tbk (KLBF) sold 4.04 million shares of PT Enseval Putera Megatrading Tbk (EPMT), reducing its ownership stake from 92.33% to 92.18%, and other companies saw changes in share ownership.
title: Multiple Indonesian companies see changes in ownership stakes through recent share purchases and sales.

body: PT Green Power Group Tbk (GREEN) plans to increase its capital through a pre-emptive rights issue in the first half of 2025, with an estimated value of IDR 100-150 billion. The company is also expanding strategically, including establishing three new subsidiaries and a 5-megawatt solar photovoltaic power plant project in Oecusse, East Nusa Tenggara, as part of its vision to make renewable energy a main pillar of its business growth.
title: PT Green Power Group to Raise IDR 100-150 Billion via Rights Issue, Eyes Strategic Expansion and Renewable Energy Projects.

body: PT Pan Brothers Tbk (PANB) has avoided bankruptcy after the Central Jakarta Commercial Court ratified a homologation agreement with its majority creditors, allowing the company to restructure its $340 million debt through a mandatory convertible bond scheme, reducing its debt to $140 million. This move is expected to strengthen Pan Brothers' financial position and support its business sustainability, with similar decisions applying to its two subsidiaries, PT Eco Smart Garment Indonesia and PT Prima Sejati Sejahtera.
title: Pan Brothers avoids bankruptcy after Jakarta court approves debt restructuring agreement with creditors.

body: PT Petrosea TBK (PTRO) will implement a 1:10 stock split, reducing its nominal value from IDR 50 to IDR 5 per share, aiming to attract retail investors with more affordable prices, effective January 7, 2025. The stock split, approved by shareholders on December 16, 2024, is expected to provide wider opportunities for individual investors to participate in PTRO's share ownership.
title: PT Petrosea TBK to Implement 1:10 Stock Split to Attract Retail Investors with Lower Share Price

body: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk (TOWR) will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 300.22 billion, with each share receiving IDR 6, to be paid on January 22, 2025, following approval from the company's board of directors and commissioners. Despite this, the company's share price has fallen 13.48% over the past month, currently trading at IDR 610 per share, with a net profit of IDR 2.45 trillion and retained earnings of IDR 18.23 trillion as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara to Distribute IDR 300.22 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders.

body: PT Indomobil Sukses Internasional Tbk (IMAS), Nissan's distributor in Indonesia, clarified that there have been no talks with Honda regarding a merger plan, despite previous reports by Reuters. If the merger were to happen, it would create the third-largest automotive group in the world in terms of vehicle sales, after Toyota and Volkswagen.
title: Indomobil Sukses Internasional clarifies no merger talks between Nissan and Honda despite previous reports.

body: PT Sunterra Indonesia increased its stake in PT Cerestar Indonesia TBK to 26.3% by buying 375 million shares, while PT Kobin Keramik Industri solidified its hold on PT Cahayaputra Asa Keramik TBK with an 88.64% stake after acquiring 314 million shares. Meanwhile, other notable transactions included CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd purchasing 112 million shares of PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur TBK and selling 1.18 million shares of PT Indonesian Paradise Property.
title: Multiple Indonesian companies see changes in ownership stakes through recent share transactions.
